steps.title: Show the old survey is outdated
steps.content: The researcher argues that a ten-year-old survey no longer reflects today's residents. The passage says willingness to walk to transit depends on factors such as sidewalks and crosswalks, so a major change in those factors would support the claim.

steps.title: Reject irrelevant changes
steps.content: Choice B describes rainy-day transit use but not a ten-year change in the neighborhood. Choice C concerns cyclists, not willingness to walk. Choice D concerns current riders' satisfaction with the line. A substantial increase in sidewalks and crosswalks makes the old walking survey less reliable. The answer is A.
